nodes.label: Pirapire Analytics Platform
nodes.rationale: League of Legends analytics platform combining upcoming matches with odds and competitive statistics from historical data.

nodes.label: Pirapire Statistics Model
nodes.rationale: Computes team and player metrics from the last 10 completed series per team strictly before the target match kickoff, with coverage labels (complete, partial, N/D).

nodes.label: Graphify Knowledge Graph Tool
nodes.rationale: Turns any folder of code, docs, papers, images, or video into a queryable knowledge graph with community detection, an honest audit trail, and multiple output formats.

nodes.label: Graphify Extraction Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Multi-step pipeline: detect files, transcribe media, extract AST (deterministic) + semantic (LLM), merge, build graph, cluster, analyze god nodes and surprising connections, generate report, label communities, produce outputs.

nodes.label: AST Structural Extraction
nodes.rationale: Deterministic, free extraction of code files using AST analysis - no LLM needed. Handles imports, calls, class hierarchy.

nodes.label: Semantic Extraction (LLM)
nodes.rationale: LLM-based extraction for docs, papers, and images. Uses Gemini if API key is set, otherwise falls back to the host agent as the LLM. Code-only corpora skip this entirely.

nodes.label: Graphify Audit Trail (EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS)
nodes.rationale: Every edge carries a confidence tag (EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS) providing an honest audit trail so the user knows what evidence supports each relationship.

nodes.label: Gemini API Backend
nodes.rationale: No API key is required for graphify. Gemini is optional for semantic extraction; the host agent itself falls back as the LLM when no key is set.

nodes.label: Graphify Fast Path (Existing Graph)
nodes.rationale: When graph.json already exists, skip extraction/build and jump to query/path/explain immediately. Avoids rebuild for questions against an existing graph.

nodes.label: Graphify Graph Shrink Guard
nodes.rationale: Prevents graph.json from being overwritten by a smaller graph (#479). Only allows the build if the new graph is at least as large as the existing one.

nodes.label: Graphify Confidence Score Rubric
nodes.rationale: Discrete confidence score tiers for INFERRED edges (0.95, 0.85, 0.75, 0.65, 0.55) to avoid bimodal distribution collapse at 0.5. EXTRACTED=1.0, AMBIGUOUS=0.1-0.3.

nodes.label: Graphify Node ID Format Convention
nodes.rationale: Node IDs use full repo-relative path stems joined with underscores, keeping every directory level so same-named files in different directories stay distinct. Never append chunk numbers.

nodes.label: Graphify Hyperedges
nodes.rationale: Capture shared concepts/flows/patterns among 3+ nodes that are not captured by pairwise edges alone. Used sparingly, max 3 per chunk.

nodes.label: Graphify Semantic Similarity Edge
nodes.rationale: Links two concepts that solve the same problem without any structural link (no import, call, or citation). Confidence 0.6-0.95.

nodes.label: Graphify source_file Verbatim Rule
nodes.rationale: source_file must be verbatim from FILE_LIST, never shortened. This keeps the full build and incremental --update on the same base so build_merge's replace-on-re-extract matches the existing node.

nodes.label: Graphify Cross-Language Call Guard
nodes.rationale: calls edges MUST stay within one language; a Python function cannot call a JS/TS/Go/Rust/Java symbol. Cross-language call edges are phantom artifacts.

nodes.label: Graphify Constrained Query Expansion
nodes.rationale: Expand user query tokens against actual graph vocabulary before traversal to prevent terminology mismatch (e.g., 'authentication' vs 'Guardian') from collapsing the answer to noise. Tokens must come from the vocab, never invented.

nodes.label: Graphify save-result Feedback Loop
nodes.rationale: Self-improving loop: saves query results back into the graph with outcomes (useful, dead_end, corrected) so future sessions learn preferred sources, dead ends, and corrections.

nodes.label: Graphify Work Memory (Self-Improving Loop)
nodes.rationale: Outcome tagging (useful/dead_end/corrected) allows future sessions to learn from past queries: preferred sources are prioritized, dead ends are skipped, and corrections override wrong prior answers.

nodes.label: Graphify LESSONS.md Reflections
nodes.rationale: Deterministic reflection output listing preferred sources, known dead ends, and prior corrections. Kept current by git hook or manual `reflect --if-stale`.

nodes.label: Graphify Incremental Update (--update)
nodes.rationale: Re-extracts only changed files since last run. Uses detect_incremental to find new/changed/deleted files, then build_merge to merge into existing graph while pruning deleted sources.

nodes.label: Graphify Code-Only Update Optimization
nodes.rationale: When all changed files are code files, skip semantic extraction entirely (no LLM needed). Only run AST extraction and merge. Saves tokens and time.

nodes.label: Graphify Cluster-Only Mode (--cluster-only)
nodes.rationale: Re-runs clustering on the existing graph without re-extraction. Self-contained: re-clusters, names communities, and regenerates GRAPH_REPORT.md, graph.json, and graph.html.

nodes.label: Graphify Watch Mode (--watch)
nodes.rationale: Background watcher that monitors a folder for file changes and auto-rebuilds the graph. Code changes trigger AST-only rebuilds (no LLM); doc/image changes write a flag for manual --update.

nodes.label: Graphify Cross-Repo Graph Merge
nodes.rationale: Merges graphs from multiple repos or subfolders into one cross-repo graph. Each node carries a repo attribute so you can filter by origin.

nodes.label: Graphify Git Post-Commit Hook
nodes.rationale: Auto-rebuilds the graph after every git commit. Detects changed code files via git diff HEAD~1, re-runs AST extraction, rebuilds graph.json and GRAPH_REPORT.md.

nodes.label: Graphify CLAUDE.md Native Integration
nodes.rationale: Writes a graphify section to project CLAUDE.md so Claude Code checks the graph before answering questions and rebuilds after code changes.

nodes.label: Graphify Whisper Transcription
nodes.rationale: Transcribes video/audio files to text using Whisper with a domain-hint prompt derived from existing graph god nodes. Transcripts are then treated as doc files in semantic extraction.

nodes.label: Graphify Honesty Rules
nodes.rationale: Core design principles: never invent an edge, never skip corpus check warning, always show token cost, never hide cohesion scores, never run HTML viz on graphs > 5000 nodes without warning.
